title: Midnight-Craving Mexican Snapper Burrito
description: A decadent, sugar-free-friendly breakfast burrito filled with tender snapper fish, black beans, and a hint of chipotle peppers in adobo sauce. Perfect for a midnight craving.
instructions: Preheat a large skillet or griddle over medium-high heat.
Spray the skillet with cooking spray and sauté the chipotle peppers until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the black beans to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Add the cooked snapper fillets to the skillet and cook, breaking up with a spoon, until heated through, about 2-3 minutes.
Warm the corn tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the burritos by spooning the fish and bean mixture onto the center of each tortilla, followed by a sprinkle of cheese, a dollop of sour cream, and a sprinkle of cilantro.
Roll the burritos tightly and serve immediately.
Serve with a squeeze of lime juice and a sprinkle of cumin.
Cooking time may vary depending on the thickness of the snapper fillets.
For an extra crunchy burrito, cook the tortillas in a dry skillet for 30-60 seconds on each side before filling and rolling.
For an extra spicy burrito, add more chipotle peppers or use hot sauce to taste.
For a vegetarian burrito, omit the snapper fillets and add roasted vegetables, such as bell peppers and onions.
For a vegan burrito, omit the cheese and sour cream and add roasted vegetables, such as bell peppers and onions.
For a gluten-free burrito, use gluten-free tortillas and be mindful of cross-contamination with gluten-containing ingredients.
For a high-protein burrito, add beans, cheese, and sour cream for added protein.
For a low-fat burrito, use low-fat cheese and sour cream, and omit the cheese altogether for an even lower-fat option.
For a low-carb burrito, use low-carb tortillas or substitute with lettuce wraps for a low-carb option.
For a low-sodium burrito, omit the salt and use low-sodium cheese and sour cream.
For a spicy burrito, add more chipotle peppers or use hot sauce to taste.
For a vegetarian burrito, omit the snapper fillets and add roasted vegetables, such as bell peppers and onions.
For a vegan burrito, omit the cheese and sour cream and add roasted vegetables, such as bell peppers and onions.
